tool.warning: rk_morph emits deterministic text templates; it is not execution of the Morph proof-search runtime.

target: A proof-neutral ZRM semantic plane preserves exact accounting, ordered manifests, context-indexed continuity, deterministic serial refinement, explicit privacy leakage, and the semantic-proof versus durable-commit authority cut across replaceable evidence backends.

kernel_candidates.statement: State an explicit equivalence between recursive acceptance and sequential manifest folding under one validation context, with durable commit excluded from both sides.
kernel_candidates.semantic_preservation_test: Enumerate bounded valid and invalid traces and compare both predicates exactly.

kernel_candidates.statement: Search for the shortest ordered manifest whose recursive grouping changes definedness, summary, predecessor continuity, or rejection reason.
kernel_candidates.semantic_preservation_test: Require the smallest counterexample and replay it under every binary tree shape.

kernel_candidates.statement: Every recursive node preserves the exact ordered leaf manifest, validation-context identity, total summary carrier, disjointness obligations, and predecessor-to-successor state relation.
kernel_candidates.semantic_preservation_test: Seed omission, duplication, reordering, context substitution, intermediate identity, and overlap mutants.

kernel_candidates.statement: Define assumptions, public inputs, private witnesses, guarantees, reject cases, and non-authority boundaries before selecting a proof system.
kernel_candidates.semantic_preservation_test: Map every field to one theorem obligation and one executable substitution test.

kernel_candidates.statement: Enumerate adversarial proof trees, manifests, contexts, catalogs, policy roots, state roots, effect commitments, and commit-evidence substitutions.
kernel_candidates.semantic_preservation_test: One field change must either change the statement or be rejected; no verifier callback may mint commit authority.

architecture_reformulations.from: binary summaries are the semantic object
architecture_reformulations.to: the ordered manifest is the semantic object; binary nodes are caches that prove equality to its fold
architecture_reformulations.expected_gain: Tree independence becomes a refinement theorem rather than an assumption about every binary composer.
architecture_reformulations.risk: A full manifest may be too large as public data; commit to it and prove exact private opening or incremental binding.

architecture_reformulations.to: CanonicalSet for within-transition coverage and OrderedTrace for postcommit history
architecture_reformulations.expected_gain: The type system blocks accidental sorting of noncommutative history.
architecture_reformulations.risk: Adapters must make every conversion explicit and prove whether it preserves or intentionally discards order.

architecture_reformulations.to: a governed, versioned acceleration-input profile authenticated by the statement or replaced by verified recomputation
architecture_reformulations.expected_gain: Optimizations remain explicit, substitutable, and composable with the ZRM validation context.
architecture_reformulations.risk: Profile governance and witness-update costs may erase the speedup; benchmark against verified recomputation.

architecture_reformulations.from: claim promotion from tool status and replay strings
architecture_reformulations.to: promotion from content-addressed receipts verified by an allowlisted checker
architecture_reformulations.risk: Receipt schemas can become another authority surface; version and minimize them.

architecture_reformulations.to: semantic receipt, private atomic commit plan, and postcommit commit-authority evidence are separate phases
architecture_reformulations.expected_gain: Preserves fail-closed authority under crash, retry, and concurrent execution.
architecture_reformulations.risk: More interfaces require exact context and statement binding across phases.

architecture_reformulations.from: bounded signed net is recursively added
architecture_reformulations.to: monotone debit and credit limbs are recursively merged; signed net is a boundary projection
architecture_reformulations.expected_gain: Checked definedness no longer depends on proof-tree parenthesization and cancelling rows retain coverage.
architecture_reformulations.risk: Limb bounds can be stricter than a net bound and require explicit dimension-level sizing.

architecture_reformulations.from: all recursive children share one immutable context forever
architecture_reformulations.to: ordinary steps preserve context and a separately governed indexed bridge changes it
architecture_reformulations.expected_gain: Long proof chains can cross policy and verifier upgrades without fact reinterpretation.
architecture_reformulations.risk: Bridge semantics become a high-assurance authority surface and must be rare, explicit, and replay-safe.

architecture_reformulations.from: parallelism is certified by disjoint nullifiers or writes
architecture_reformulations.to: speculation refines a canonical serial order through sealed exact point and range observations
architecture_reformulations.expected_gain: Write skew, policy dependencies, range phantoms, and worker-timing nondeterminism become testable failures.
architecture_reformulations.risk: Exact read instrumentation and reexecution can reduce throughput; retain a governed I-confluent fast path only with stronger proof.

architecture_reformulations.to: semantic segment hash identifies economic and replay meaning; proof-node identity separately binds backend, release, and topology
architecture_reformulations.expected_gain: Alternative encodings and topologies cannot redeem one semantic segment twice.
architecture_reformulations.risk: The projection must be exact and proof-system malleability still needs a cryptographic review.

architecture_reformulations.from: a mutable scalar status summarizes research confidence
architecture_reformulations.to: immutable evidence and promotion receipts derive four-valued truth plus an orthogonal assurance vector at a checkpoint and policy version
architecture_reformulations.expected_gain: Negative evidence compounds, disagreement remains visible, and signed or reproducible false claims are not mislabeled proved.
architecture_reformulations.risk: Typed policy and exact scope comparison add complexity; begin with deterministic bytes and a small verifier.

strict_replay_requirements: Pin the Morph commit, dependency environment, domain constructor, runtime constructor, tactics, strategy, bounds, and seeds.
Require a verifier-owned trusted constructor registry for custom ZRM domains.
Require verification_level replay, not shape or artifact validation.
Bind any Lean certificate to an allowlisted module, declaration, statement hash, source root, and toolchain root.
Reject sorryAx and undeclared axioms by inspecting declaration dependencies.
non_claims: These are search reformulations, not accepted architecture decisions.
The full Morph runtime did not run in this environment.
A Lean source file that merely elaborates is not automatically a protocol-bound certificate.
